title: derris polyphylla
intro: Derris polyphylla, also known as Kanthari, is a plant species native to tropical regions, particularly in the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. It is a legume with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including snake bites, fever, and respiratory issues. The plant's roots and leaves are rich in alkaloids, such as roemerol and polyphyllin, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, anti-pyretic, and anti-septic properties, offering several health benefits, including pain relief and wound healing. In traditional medicine, Derris polyphylla is prepared in various herbal forms, including decoctions, infusions, and poultices, which are applied topically or consumed as teas and tonics to harness its medicinal properties.
